title: Getting Started
description: Install Howl and create your first full-stack app

blocks.text: Howl requires Deno 2.x. No Node.js or npm needed.
Add Howl to your project using the JSR package registry.
This adds the package to your deno.json imports. You will also want the dev tools:
Howl uses a file-system convention. A typical project looks like:

my-app/
  server/
    main.ts          ← Howl app entry
    apis/
      public/
        ping.api.ts  ← API route
  client/
    pages/
      _app.tsx       ← HTML shell
      _layout.tsx    ← shared layout
      index.tsx      ← home page
    islands/
      counter.island.tsx
  static/
    style.css
  howl.config.ts     ← shared config & types
  dev.ts             ← dev server entry
The server/ and client/ split is recommended but not required. You can keep everything flat for smaller apps.
The config file defines your State type, roles, and exports a typed defineApi helper.
Create the Howl app, wire middleware, register API routes, then serve client pages.
Create dev.ts to start the HowlBuilder, which handles esbuild, CSS, and hot reload.
Add --watch to dev.ts to enable auto-restart on server file changes.
